questions.question: The newly discovered asteroid exhibits a highly ______ orbit, deviating significantly from the typical circular paths of other celestial bodies in its vicinity.

options.A: predictable
options.B: eccentric
options.C: uniform
options.D: monotonous
options.E: stationary

questions.question: Despite the rigorous safety protocols, the chemical spill caused ______ damage to the local ecosystem, leading to a decade-long restoration project.

options.A: negligible
options.B: transient
options.C: deleterious
options.D: superficial
options.E: innocuous

questions.question: The researcher's findings were so ______ that they fundamentally altered the scientific community's understanding of neurological regeneration.

options.A: groundbreaking
options.B: obsolete
options.C: ambiguous
options.D: redundant
options.E: trivial

questions.question: Although the global economy showed signs of recovery in the first quarter, ______.

options.A: the unemployment rate continued to climb in several major industrial hubs
options.B: it has reached its highest peak in the last twenty years
options.C: because investors were optimistic about the future of tech stocks
options.D: it will have been a great success for the emerging markets
options.E: most analysts believe that inflation will be eliminated immediately

questions.question: ______ that many civilizations in the past collapsed due to unsustainable agricultural practices.

options.A: Historical evidence strongly suggests
options.B: It was hardly a surprise for the archaeologists
options.C: Unless we change our current consumption patterns
options.D: As long as the soil remains fertile and productive
options.E: Despite the technological advancements of the era

questions.question: While some experts argue that artificial intelligence will replace human labor, ______.

options.A: others maintain that it will create entirely new categories of employment
options.B: it is already being used in many industrial manufacturing processes
options.C: it was developed to solve complex mathematical problems
options.D: they are concerned about the ethical implications of data privacy
options.E: the software has failed to meet the expected performance benchmarks

questions.question: Many species are facing extinction today ______.

options.A: because their natural habitats are being destroyed at an alarming rate
options.B: even if the conservation efforts were successful
options.C: so that they can adapt to the changing climatic conditions
options.D: as if they had no role in the ecological balance
options.E: in case the government decides to expand the protected areas

questions.question: Cloze Test Passage: The concept of emotional intelligence (4) ______ significant attention in the field of psychology since the 1990s. It refers to the ability to recognize and manage one's own emotions, (5) ______ those of others. Proponents argue that high EQ is (6) ______ for leadership success. (7) ______, critics suggest that the concept lacks a rigorous empirical foundation. Regardless of the debate, many corporations now (8) ______ EQ assessments into their hiring processes. Question 4:

options.A: has garnered
options.B: will garner
options.C: is garnering
options.D: had garnered
options.E: garners

questions.question: Question 5 (refer to the previous passage):

options.A: as well as
options.B: rather than
options.C: instead of
options.D: but for
options.E: in spite of

questions.question: Question 6 (refer to the previous passage):

options.A: indispensable
options.B: irrelevant
options.C: optional
options.D: detrimental
options.E: hostile

questions.question: Question 7 (refer to the previous passage):

options.A: However
options.B: Moreover
options.C: Therefore
options.D: Similarly
options.E: Consequently

questions.question: Question 8 (refer to the previous passage):

options.A: incorporate
options.B: eliminate
options.C: abandon
options.D: prohibit
options.E: neglect

questions.question: Modern tıp, hastalıkların tedavisinden ziyade %C3%B6nlenmesine odaklanarak insan %C3%B6mr%C3%BCn%C3%BC %C3%B6nemli %C3%B6l%C3%A7%C3%BCde uzatmışır.

options.A: Modern medicine has significantly extended human life by focusing on the prevention rather than the treatment of diseases.
options.B: Human life has been extended by modern medicine which focuses both on prevention and treatment.
options.C: Modern medicine focuses on treating diseases to extend human life significantly.
options.D: The prevention of diseases is more important than treatment in modern medicine to extend life.
options.E: Because modern medicine focuses on prevention, human life expectancy has increased.

questions.question: The industrial revolution not only changed production methods but also reshaped the social structure of the entire world.

options.A: Sanayi devrimi sadece %C3%BCretim y%C3%B6ntemlerini de%C4%9iştirmedi, aynı zamanda t%C3%BCm d%C3%BCnyanın sosyal yapısını da yeniden %C5%9ekillendirdi.
options.B: Sanayi devrimi %C3%BCretim y%C3%B6ntemlerini de%C4%9iştirirken d%C3%BCnyanın sosyal yapısını da etkiledi.
options.C: Hem %C3%BCretim y%C3%B6ntemleri hem de sosyal yapı sanayi devrimi ile tamamen de%C4%9işmiştir.
options.D: Sanayi devriminin en b%C3%BCy%C3%BCk etkisi sosyal yapıyı ve %C3%BCretim ara%C3%A7larını de%C4%9iştirmesi olmuştur.
options.E: D%C3%BCnya genelindeki sosyal yapı, sanayi devriminin %C3%BCretim y%C3%B6ntemlerini de%C4%9iştirmesiyle başka bir hal aldı.

questions.question: K%C3%BCresel ısınma ile m%C3%BCcadele etmek i%C3%A7in h%C3%BCk%C3%BCmetlerin karbon emisyonlarını azaltmaya y%C3%B6nelik radikal adımlar atması gerekmektedir.

options.A: Governments must take radical steps to reduce carbon emissions in order to combat global warming.
options.B: In order to stop global warming, governments are reducing carbon emissions with radical steps.
options.C: Taking radical steps against carbon emissions is how governments should fight global warming.
options.D: Global warming can only be fought if governments reduce their carbon emissions radically.
options.E: Radical steps taken by governments will help reduce carbon emissions and global warming.

questions.question: Scientists have found that regular exercise not only improves physical health but also boosts cognitive functions in the elderly.

options.A: Bilim insanları, d%C3%BCzenli egzersizin sadece fiziksel sağığı iyileştirmediğini, aynı zamanda yaşşılarda bilişsel fonksiyonları da artırdğını bulmuştur.
options.B: Egzersiz yapmanın yaşşılarda hem fiziksel hem de zihinsel faydaları olduğunu bilim insanları kanıtladı.
options.C: D%C3%BCzenli egzersiz yapan yaşşıların bilişsel ve fiziksel sağıklarının daha iyi olduğunu bilim insanları s%C3%B6yl%C3%BCyor.
options.D: Bilim insanlarına g%C3%B6re, d%C3%BCzenli egzersiz fiziksel sağıkla beraber yaşşıların zihnini de g%C3%BC%C3%A7lendiriyor.
options.E: Yaşşılarda bilişsel gelişim i%C3%A7in d%C3%BCzenli egzersizin %C5%9art olduğunu bilim insanları belirtiyor.

questions.question: It is not until a child starts school that their social circle begins to expand significantly beyond the family unit.

options.A: A child's social life remains limited to the family until they begin their formal education.
options.B: Children start making friends only after they have spent several years in school.
options.C: The family unit is the most important social circle for a child even after they start school.
options.D: Starting school is the only way for a child to develop social skills effectively.
options.E: Schooling has little impact on the social development of a child compared to family.

questions.question: Had the government implemented the economic reforms earlier, the current financial crisis might have been averted.

options.A: The current financial crisis is a direct result of the government's failure to act in time with economic reforms.
options.B: The government did implement reforms, but they were not enough to stop the financial crisis.
options.C: If the government had not implemented reforms, the financial crisis would have been much worse.
options.D: The financial crisis was unavoidable regardless of when the economic reforms were introduced.
options.E: Governments usually fail to prevent financial crises even if they implement reforms early.

questions.question: No sooner had the team published their results than other researchers began to challenge their methodology.

options.A: As soon as the results were released, the team's methods came under scrutiny from other scientists.
options.B: The team published their results after they were sure that no one would challenge their methodology.
options.C: Other researchers had been challenging the methodology long before the results were published.
options.D: The team's methodology was so perfect that no other researchers could challenge it initially.
options.E: The team delayed the publication of their results because they feared their methodology would be challenged.

questions.question: Rarely do we find a literary work that manages to capture the complexities of human nature as accurately as this novel.

options.A: This novel is exceptional in its precise portrayal of the intricate aspects of being human.
options.B: It is common for novels to explore human nature, but this one is slightly more accurate.
options.C: Human nature is so complex that no novel can ever capture it entirely accurately.
options.D: We often find literary works that are just as good as this novel in describing human nature.
options.E: This novel fails to capture human nature, unlike many other works of literature.

questions.question:
Arthur: Have you seen the latest report on renewable energy adoption in Europe? 

 Beatrice: Yes, it seems that offshore wind farms are becoming the primary source of power in the North Sea. 

 Arthur: ______ 

 Beatrice: True, but the long-term environmental benefits and energy independence far outweigh those initial obstacles.

options.A: That's impressive, but I'm worried about the high costs of maintenance and installation.
options.B: I think solar energy is a much more viable option for southern European countries.
options.C: The report also mentions that coal usage is at an all-time low across the continent.
options.D: Why do you think governments are subsidizing these projects so heavily?
options.E: The efficiency of wind turbines has improved significantly over the last decade.

questions.question:
Dr. Miller: The patient's response to the new immunotherapy has been quite remarkable. 

 Nurse Sarah: Indeed, the tumor size has decreased by forty percent in just three weeks. 

 Dr. Miller: ______ 

 Nurse Sarah: Exactly. We should continue the current dosage and monitor the liver enzymes closely.

options.A: We must remain cautious about potential side effects as we proceed with the treatment.
options.B: I believe we should switch to traditional chemotherapy to ensure a full recovery.
options.C: The patient has expressed a desire to be discharged as soon as possible.
options.D: How many other patients are currently enrolled in this specific clinical trial?
options.E: I'm surprised that the insurance company agreed to cover the cost of this therapy.

questions.question:
Professor: The Gini coefficient is a crucial tool for measuring income inequality within a nation. 

 Student: But doesn't it have limitations when comparing countries with different cost of living? 

 Professor: ______ 

 Student: I see. So, we should look at it alongside other indicators like the Human Development Index.

options.A: You're right; it doesn't account for the absolute wealth or purchasing power of the citizens.
options.B: The Gini coefficient is the only reliable way to understand economic disparities.
options.C: Most economists prefer to use the GDP per capita instead of the Gini coefficient.
options.D: Income inequality is actually decreasing in most developing nations according to recent data.
options.E: The calculation of the coefficient is quite simple once you have the Lorenz curve.

questions.question:
Journalist: Minister, how do you respond to the allegations of corruption within the housing department? 

 Minister: We have initiated a full internal audit to investigate every transaction made in the last fiscal year. 

 Journalist: ______ 

 Minister: As soon as the audit is finalized, the results will be made available to the public without exception.

options.A: Will the findings of this investigation be transparent and accessible to the press?
options.B: Who is leading the audit team and what are their qualifications for this task?
options.C: Does this mean the previous housing projects will be suspended until further notice?
options.D: How much money is estimated to have been misappropriated by the officials?
options.E: The public is demanding your resignation; do you have any comments on that?

questions.question: The Silk Road was not merely a single path but a network of trade routes connecting the East and the West. It facilitated the exchange of goods like silk, spices, and precious metals. ______. For instance, Buddhism spread from India to China and beyond through the movements of monks and merchants along these routes.

options.A: However, it also played a vital role in the transmission of ideas, religions, and technologies.
options.B: The maritime routes eventually became more popular than the land-based Silk Road.
options.C: Many merchants faced great dangers, including bandits and harsh weather conditions.
options.D: The decline of the Silk Road was caused by the rise of the Ottoman Empire's control over trade.
options.E: Today, the concept of the New Silk Road is being revived through modern infrastructure projects.

questions.question: Quantum computing represents a paradigm shift in how we process information. Unlike classical computers that use bits, quantum computers use qubits, which can exist in multiple states simultaneously. ______. This could lead to breakthroughs in fields such as cryptography, material science, and drug discovery.

options.A: This allows them to perform complex calculations at speeds unattainable by today's supercomputers.
options.B: Building a stable quantum computer requires keeping the qubits at extremely low temperatures.
options.C: The software for quantum computers is still in its infancy and requires new programming languages.
options.D: Google and IBM are currently the leaders in the race to achieve quantum supremacy.
options.E: Many people worry that quantum computers will eventually break all current encryption methods.

questions.question: Urbanization is a global phenomenon that has transformed the way people live and work. As more people move to cities in search of better opportunities, urban infrastructure often struggles to keep pace. ______. Consequently, city planners are increasingly focusing on sustainable development and smart city technologies to address these challenges.

options.A: This rapid growth can lead to problems such as traffic congestion, pollution, and housing shortages.
options.B: Historical cities were often built near rivers to facilitate trade and provide water.
options.C: The rural population is expected to decrease significantly over the next few decades.
options.D: Living in a city offers access to better healthcare and education for most families.
options.E: The architecture of modern skyscrapers is a testament to human engineering capabilities.

questions.question: Marine biology is the study of organisms in the ocean and other saltwater environments. Given that the ocean covers more than 70% of the Earth's surface, the scope of this field is vast. ______. These ecosystems are not only home to diverse life forms but also play a critical role in regulating the planet's climate.

options.A: Researchers focus on everything from microscopic plankton to the largest whales in the sea.
options.B: The deep sea remains one of the least explored regions on our entire planet.
options.C: Overfishing and plastic pollution are the greatest threats to marine biodiversity today.
options.D: Ocean currents are responsible for distributing heat around the globe.
options.E: Many marine biologists work for government agencies or non-profit organizations.

questions.question: The committee reached a consensus after hours of heated debate regarding the new budget.

options.A: The committee finally agreed on the new budget following a long and intense discussion.
options.B: The budget was rejected by the committee because the debate lasted for several hours.
options.C: Despite the heated debate, the committee members could not agree on the budget.
options.D: The committee members were too tired to continue the debate, so they accepted the budget.
options.E: A consensus was reached quickly because the budget was well-prepared.

questions.question: It is highly probable that the experiment will yield different results under controlled laboratory conditions.

options.A: The experiment is very likely to produce different outcomes if conducted in a controlled lab.
options.B: Controlled laboratory conditions are necessary for the experiment to be successful.
options.C: The results of the experiment will definitely change if we move it to a laboratory.
options.D: There is a slight chance that the experiment might work better in a lab setting.
options.E: We should not expect any different results even if the laboratory conditions are controlled.

questions.question: Only by working together can we hope to overcome the challenges posed by climate change.

options.A: Collaborative effort is the only way we might manage to solve the problems of climate change.
options.B: If we work together, we will definitely solve all the issues related to climate change.
options.C: We have already overcome the challenges of climate change by working as a team.
options.D: Climate change poses such great challenges that even working together might not be enough.
options.E: The challenges of climate change are so simple that we don't need to work together.

questions.question: (I) The Renaissance was a period of great cultural and intellectual change in Europe. (II) It began in Italy in the 14th century and later spread to the rest of the continent. (III) During this time, there was a renewed interest in the classical art and literature of Greece and Rome. (IV) The Industrial Revolution, however, led to the rise of factory-based manufacturing. (V) Famous figures of the Renaissance include Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods. (II) It generally involves the green pigment chlorophyll and generates oxygen as a byproduct. (III) Most plants need water and carbon dioxide to perform this process effectively. (IV) Some animals, such as the chameleon, can change their color to blend into their surroundings. (V) Without photosynthesis, there would be little food or other organic matter on Earth.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Artificial intelligence is rapidly evolving and finding applications in various sectors. (II) In healthcare, AI is used to analyze medical images and predict patient outcomes. (III) Many people enjoy watching science fiction movies that feature humanoid robots. (IV) In finance, algorithms help in detecting fraudulent transactions and managing risks. (V) Transportation is also being transformed by the development of autonomous vehicles.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: Passage: The Great Barrier Reef, the world's largest coral reef system, is currently facing an unprecedented threat from rising ocean temperatures. This phenomenon, known as coral bleaching, occurs when corals under stress expel the algae living in their tissues, causing them to turn completely white. While corals can survive a bleaching event, they are under more stress and are subject to mortality. In 2016 and 2017, back-to-back bleaching events killed an estimated 50% of the Great Barrier Reef's corals. Scientists warn that if global temperatures continue to rise, these events will become more frequent and severe, potentially leading to the total collapse of the reef ecosystem. According to the passage, what is the primary cause of coral bleaching?

options.A: Rising ocean temperatures
options.B: The expulsion of predatory fish
options.C: An overabundance of algae
options.D: Pollution from coastal cities
options.E: The physical destruction of reefs by tourists

questions.question: Question: Based on the passage, what happened to the Great Barrier Reef in 2016 and 2017?

options.A: It lost about half of its coral population due to bleaching.
options.B: It was declared a protected national park to prevent further damage.
options.C: The algae population increased, helping the corals to recover.
options.D: New coral species were discovered in the deeper parts of the reef.
options.E: The temperature of the ocean decreased unexpectedly.

questions.question: Question: What is the scientist's warning regarding the future of the Great Barrier Reef?

options.A: The reef might collapse entirely if global warming is not addressed.
options.B: The reef will naturally adapt to warmer temperatures over time.
options.C: Bleaching events are a natural part of the reef's life cycle and are not dangerous.
options.D: Only the northern parts of the reef are at risk of disappearing.
options.E: New technology will be able to reverse the effects of coral bleaching.

questions.question: Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, marked a major turning point in history. Almost every aspect of daily life was influenced in some way. Average income and population began to exhibit unprecedented sustained growth. In the two centuries following 1800, the world's average per capita income increased over tenfold, while the world's population increased over sixfold. In the words of Nobel Prize winner Robert E. Lucas, Jr., 'For the first time in history, the living standards of the masses of ordinary people have begun to undergo sustained growth.' However, this period also brought about significant challenges, including poor working conditions, urban overcrowding, and environmental degradation. What was one of the major economic effects of the Industrial Revolution mentioned in the text?

options.A: A tenfold increase in the world's average per capita income
options.B: A decrease in the global population due to urban diseases
options.C: The total elimination of poverty among the working class
options.D: The stabilization of income across all social classes
options.E: The shift from international trade to local manufacturing

questions.question: Question: According to Robert E. Lucas, Jr., why was the Industrial Revolution unique?

options.A: It was the first time living standards for ordinary people grew continuously.
options.B: It marked the beginning of scientific experimentation in factories.
options.C: It led to the discovery of new continents and trade routes.
options.D: It was the only period where population growth outpaced food production.
options.E: It allowed governments to control the economy for the first time.

questions.question: Question: Which of the following was NOT mentioned as a challenge brought by the Industrial Revolution?

options.A: The depletion of natural resources in the colonies
options.B: Poor working conditions
options.C: Urban overcrowding
options.D: Environmental degradation
options.E: Societal shifts affecting daily life
